title: derris acuminata
intro: Derris acuminata, also known as the 'Toba root', is a plant species native to tropical forests in Asia and Latin America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its rotenone content, which is toxic to insects and fish but has been shown to have some health benefits when used in moderation. The rotenone in Derris acuminata is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin infections. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, but its use should be carefully considered and monitored due to its potential toxicity.
